2021-01-01.title: The U.S. reaches 20 million cases.
2021-01-01.abstract: The United States recorded its 20 millionth case since the start of the coronavirus pandemic on Thursday, surpassing a grim milestone just as the prospects for getting the virus under control quickly in the new year appeared to dim.

2021-01-01.title: Florida health officials detect the state’s first identified coronavirus variant case.
2021-01-01.abstract: A highly contagious coronavirus variant first identified in Britain has been discovered in Florida, health officials said on Thursday.

2021-01-01.title: An Embattled Public Servant in a Fractured France
2021-01-01.abstract: Nicolas Cadène sees the failings of France’s secular model even as he upholds it.

2021-01-01.title: What to Know as Troubled Afghan Peace Talks Enter a New Phase
2021-01-01.abstract: The Afghan government and the Taliban are set to continue negotiations toward a cease-fire in early January, but several fundamental issues stand in the way of progress.

2021-01-01.title: France couldn’t stop the New Year’s parties. One rave drew 2,500 people who fought off the police.
2021-01-01.abstract: Local authorities said that stones and bottles were thrown at police officers who tried to break up the party, and that a police car was set on fire.

2021-01-02.title: A Virginia state senator, Ben Chafin, has died from complications of Covid-19.
2021-01-02.abstract: He died after two weeks in a Richmond hospital.

2021-01-02.title: The New Orleans Saints’ star running back, Alvin Kamara, is placed on the N.F.L.’s Covid reserve list.
2021-01-02.abstract: Alvin Kamara, the star running back for the New Orleans Saints and one of the best players in the N.F.L. this season, has been placed on the N.F.L.’s Covid-19 reserve list, a move that will keep him out of the team’s regular-season finale on Sunday and that may jeopardize his chances to appear in the team’s first game of the playoffs.

2021-01-02.title: How Taiwan Plans to Stay (Mostly) Covid-Free
2021-01-02.abstract: The island’s success against the coronavirus has created a sinking feeling for many residents: How much longer can their good fortune last?

2021-01-02.title: Rowing the Nile: A Soothing Respite in a Chaotic Metropolis
2021-01-02.abstract: Ancient pharaohs rowed the Nile. Now Egyptians have rediscovered the practice, finding a new perspective on the river that shaped their country.

2021-01-02.title: He Calmed Gaza, Aided Israel’s Arab Ties and Preserved Hopes for Peace
2021-01-02.abstract: After six years of quiet diplomacy as the U.N.’s Middle East envoy, Nickolay Mladenov leaves his post to a chorus of praise from an unlikely choir.

2021-01-03.title: India Approves Oxford-AstraZeneca Covid-19 Vaccine and 1 Other
2021-01-03.abstract: The approvals, which include a shot developed by Bharat Biotech, an Indian pharmaceutical company, begin a vast campaign to inoculate the hard-hit nation’s 1.3 billion people.

2021-01-03.title: With Concessions and Deals, China’s Leader Tries to Box Out Biden
2021-01-03.abstract: Xi Jinping has been making trade agreements in Asia and now Europe, hoping to head off the incoming president’s efforts to rally a united front against China.

2021-01-03.title: The Julian Assange Extradition Case: What You Need to Know
2021-01-03.abstract: A British judge rejected an American effort to send the WikiLeaks founder to the U.S. to face charges of violating the Espionage Act and hacking government computers.

2021-01-03.title: Armed Attackers Kidnap and Kill 11 Miners in Pakistan
2021-01-03.abstract: Officials said all the victims were ethnic Hazaras, a Shiite minority group that Sunni extremists have often targeted.

2021-01-03.title: Italy Mourns an Immigrant’s Life Cut Short
2021-01-03.abstract: The killing of Agitu Ideo Gudeta, a successful business owner from Ethiopia who raised goats and made cheese, has resonated across the country. The police say a farmhand was responsible.

2021-01-04.title: In Reversal, Pentagon Announces Aircraft Carrier Nimitz Will Remain in Middle East
2021-01-04.abstract: The acting defense secretary abruptly reversed his previous order to redeploy the Nimitz, which he had done over the objections of his top military advisers.

2021-01-04.title: In Europe, more countries delay second vaccine doses or mull plans to do so.
2021-01-04.abstract: As governments around the world rush to vaccinate their citizens, scientists and policymakers are debating whether to reserve the second doses everyone will need, or give as many people as possible just one shot now — potentially at the expense of giving second doses on schedule.

2021-01-04.title: Iraq, Struggling to Pay Debts and Salaries, Plunges Into Economic Crisis
2021-01-04.abstract: Oil-rich Iraq, its economy hobbled by neglect and corruption, has devalued its currency and had its imported electricity cut off for nonpayment.

2021-01-04.title: The pandemic has hit addiction recovery hard.
2021-01-04.abstract: Addiction is often referred to as a disease of isolation, and overcoming that challenge has only become more difficult during a pandemic that has forced people indoors — in some cases to live lonely lives, with drugs and alcohol as a way to cope with the stress.

2021-01-04.title: U.K. Judge Blocks Assange’s Extradition to U.S., Citing Mental Health
2021-01-04.abstract: U.S. officials want the WikiLeaks founder to face charges of violating the Espionage Act. But a judge in London ruled that he was at extreme risk of suicide.

2021-01-05.title: Mexico approved the Oxford-AstraZeneca vaccine for emergency use.
2021-01-05.abstract: Mexico approved the Oxford-AstraZeneca coronavirus vaccine for emergency use on Monday, the country’s top epidemiologist, Hugo López-Gatell, announced in a tweet on Monday evening. It is the fourth country to approve the vaccine.

2021-01-05.title: The F.D.A. opposes halving Moderna vaccine doses.
2021-01-05.abstract: The Food and Drug Administration late Monday criticized an idea floated by one of the administration’s top vaccine officials for stretching the limited number of Covid-19 vaccine doses, saying that a proposal for half-doses of the Moderna vaccine was “premature and not rooted solidly in the available science.”

2021-01-05.title: With the virus subdued, China enjoys its own kind of freedom.
2021-01-05.abstract: While many countries are still reeling from Covid-19, China — where the pandemic originated — has become one of the safest places in the world. The country reported fewer than 100,000 infections for all of 2020. The United States has been reporting more than that every day since early November.

2021-01-05.title: Is the Vaccine Halal? Indonesians Await the Answer
2021-01-05.abstract: President Joko Widodo hopes to begin inoculations soon, but the vaccine from the Chinese company Sinovac still needs approval from safety regulators and an influential council of Muslim clerics.

2021-01-05.title: Vietnam Imprisons 3 Journalists Amid Broader Attack on Speech
2021-01-05.abstract: The sentences are the latest crackdown on dissent in the one-party state, as the ruling Communist Party prepares for an important political conclave.

2021-01-06.title: As the coronavirus rages in California, L.A. medics are told to ration oxygen.
2021-01-06.abstract: Officials in California have painted an increasingly catastrophic picture of the state’s Covid-19 crisis over the past several days, precisely what they had warned was coming, as the state faces an oxygen shortage.

2021-01-06.title: North Korea Party Congress Opens With Kim Jong-un Admitting Failures
2021-01-06.abstract: Mr. Kim convened the first Workers’ Party Congress in five years to revamp his policies as North Korea’s economic troubles deepened amid international sanctions and the pandemic.

2021-01-06.title: Hong Kong Police Arrest Dozens of Pro-Democracy Leaders
2021-01-06.abstract: The 53 officials and activists were detained under the national security law for their efforts to choose candidates to run in the city’s legislative elections.

2021-01-06.title: Did Oxygen Outage Kill Covid Patients in Egypt? Government Says No
2021-01-06.abstract: With anger swelling over a video clip purporting to show patients dying in a hospital after their oxygen was cut off, the authorities offered a counternarrative.

2021-01-06.title: Iran Moves to Outlaw Sexual Violence and Harassment of Women
2021-01-06.abstract: An emerging #MeToo movement and public horror over “honor killings” helped spur the government’s bill, but it still must be adopted by the country’s conservative Parliament.

2021-01-07.title: A Quarantine Nightmare: Three Weeks in a Hotel, if You Can Find One
2021-01-07.abstract: Hong Kong’s abrupt new entry requirement — which has shaken travelers, domestic workers, airlines and hotels alike — stands out for its length and has been questioned by scientists.

2021-01-07.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in January
2021-01-07.abstract: Our streaming picks for January, including ‘Cobra Kai,’ ‘Bump’ and ‘One Night in Miami …’

2021-01-07.title: Japan Declares State of Emergency in Tokyo Area After Days of Hesitation
2021-01-07.abstract: Fast-rising coronavirus case counts and deaths forced Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga to act, but some say the restrictions — mostly voluntary — won’t be enough.

2021-01-07.title: Overshadowed by events in Washington, the virus was deadlier than ever.
2021-01-07.abstract: As Americans were transfixed by the spectacle of the Capitol under siege, the coronavirus continued to sweep across the United States.

2021-01-07.title: Study says plasma infusions, administered early, can keep older patients from many severe effects.
2021-01-07.abstract: Blood plasma from recovered Covid-19 patients can help older adults avoid getting seriously sick with the coronavirus — if the therapy is administered within days of the onset of the illness, a small but rigorous clinical trial in Argentina found.

2021-01-08.title: More states are detecting the highly contagious virus variant.
2021-01-08.abstract: The list of states to identify the dangerous new coronavirus variant is growing.

2021-01-08.title: Muslim Cleric Tied to Bali Nightclub Bombing Is Freed From Prison
2021-01-08.abstract: Abu Bakar Bashir, 82, the spiritual leader of an extremist group that bombed churches, hotels and a Bali nightclub in the 2000s, is released after 10 years behind bars.

2021-01-08.title: Rapid virus testing will ramp up inthe U.S., as F.D.A. approved three new at-home kits.
2021-01-08.abstract: Amid surging coronavirus cases, the top U.S. testing official on Thursday announced another scale-up in the country’s diagnostic efforts, touting the importance of tests that can run from start to finish outside the lab.

2021-01-08.title: California is loosening its vaccine eligibility rules.
2021-01-08.abstract: California, confronting its worst surge of coronavirus infections yet, is loosening its vaccine protocol in an effort to avoid wasting Covid-19 vaccines.

2021-01-08.title: South Korean Court Orders Japan to Pay Compensation for Wartime Sexual Slavery
2021-01-08.abstract: The ruling, which the Japanese government rejected, is likely to aggravate already chilly relations between the two key allies of the United States.

2021-01-09.title: Kim Jong-un Vows to Boost North Korea’s Nuclear Capability as Leverage With Biden
2021-01-09.abstract: But as he pledged to advance the country’s weaponry, the North Korean leader also said he did “not rule out diplomacy.”

2021-01-09.title: As Coronavirus Mutates, the World Stumbles Again to Respond
2021-01-09.abstract: A South African tip led to the discovery of mutations around the world. With infections skyrocketing, “it’s a race against time.”

2021-01-09.title: Indonesian Jetliner Crashes Into the Sea After Takeoff, Carrying 62
2021-01-09.abstract: The Boeing 737-500, a Sriwijaya Air flight, had taken off from Jakarta, the capital. Officials said they found body parts and possibly part of the wreckage.

2021-01-09.title: Asia’s Weather Was Frigid, but the Pictures Are Nice
2021-01-09.abstract: In Seoul, Beijing and elsewhere, temperatures neared or exceeded record lows this week.

2021-01-09.title: In Vaccine Geopolitics, a Great Game Played With Ukrainians’ Health
2021-01-09.abstract: Blocked from obtaining vaccines from the United States, its putative ally, and taunted in Russian propaganda, Ukraine turned to China.

2021-01-10.title: At Least 12 Dead in 2 Landslides in Indonesia
2021-01-10.abstract: The landslides on the island of Java were set off by heavy rainfall and unstable soil, officials said, and left rescue workers searching for survivors.

2021-01-10.title: Strong Views and ‘Close to the Boss’: How U.S. Envoy Reshaped a Conflict
2021-01-10.abstract: David Friedman, President Trump’s bankruptcy lawyer-turned-ambassador, leaves his post having radically overhauled policy toward Israel and the Palestinians.

2021-01-10.title: At Least 4 Dead in Shooting Spree in Chicago and Evanston
2021-01-10.abstract: A rampage that started in Chicago and ended in nearby Evanston left at least four people dead, including the suspect. At least four others were critically wounded.

2021-01-10.title: Indonesia Crash Thwarts Push to Rehabilitate Country’s Airlines
2021-01-10.abstract: The loss of Sriwijaya Air Flight 182 on Saturday was a grim start to the year in a sprawling archipelago nation where barely a year goes by without an air disaster.

2021-01-10.title: A Year After Wuhan, China Tells a Tale of Triumph (and No Mistakes)
2021-01-10.abstract: The Chinese Communist Party’s efforts to hide its missteps have taken on new urgency as the anniversary of the world’s first Covid-19 lockdown nears.

2021-01-11.title: Catch a Fish in Paris. Post on Social Media. Release.
2021-01-11.abstract: A new, younger generation of fishers is taking over the banks of the Seine, transforming a centuries-old tradition into an underground culture.

2021-01-11.title: More New Yorkers start receiving the Covid-19 vaccine.
2021-01-11.abstract: New York on Monday began giving vaccines to residents 75 and older as well as a wider range of essential workers, as state health officials expanded the group of people eligible to receive the vaccine.

2021-01-11.title: On Taiwan’s New Passport, the Incredible Shrinking ‘Republic of China’
2021-01-11.abstract: Officials said the redesign was an attempt to disassociate Taiwanese citizens from those on the mainland, who faced travel restrictions amid the pandemic.

2021-01-11.title: 6 Park Rangers Ambushed and Killed in Congo Gorilla Reserve
2021-01-11.abstract: The attack in the Virunga National Park was the latest assault on rangers charged with protecting one of Africa’s oldest nature areas.

2021-01-11.title: Indonesia gives emergency approval to a vaccine from a Chinese company.
2021-01-11.abstract: Indonesia  authorized on Monday the emergency use of a Covid-19 vaccine made by a private Chinese company, after late-stage clinical data reaffirmed that the shot is effective. The step cleared the way for a mass vaccination program to begin in the country.

2021-01-12.title: Slouch or Slack Off, This ‘Smart’ Office Chair Cushion Will Record It
2021-01-12.abstract: A Chinese tech company designed a way to track employees’ health, but sensors were also monitoring when they were away from their desks, setting off a debate about privacy and surveillance.

2021-01-12.title: Gorillas at a San Diego Zoo park tested positive for the coronavirus.
2021-01-12.abstract: Several gorillas at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park have tested positive for the coronavirus, becoming what federal officials say are the first known apes in the United States to be infected.

2021-01-12.title: Here’s what to know about the South African and U.K. virus variants.
2021-01-12.abstract: In recent weeks, scientists have raised concerns about a coronavirus variant first detected in December in South Africa, noting that this version of the virus may spread more quickly than its cousins, and perhaps be harder to quash with current vaccines.

2021-01-12.abstract: Three members of Congress who have tested positive for the coronavirus this week say they believe their infections are linked to their time spent in a secure location with colleagues who did not wear masks during the rampage last week in the U.S. Capitol.

2021-01-12.abstract: Farmers declared the temporary halt of market-oriented agriculture laws a “trick” to ease the pressure they have been putting on the government of Narendra Modi.

2021-01-13.title: Cuba, Though Angered by Terror Designation, Is Looking Past Trump
2021-01-13.abstract: The U.S. decision was made in “the death throes of a failed and corrupt administration,” Cuba’s president said. For Cubans, the change in administrations in Washington can’t come soon enough.

2021-01-13.title: ​Kim Jong-un Uses Party ​Congress to Double Down on Nuclear Program
2021-01-13.abstract: The North Korean leader said his economic policies had failed, but he called his nuclear arms buildup one of the great feats “in the history of the Korean nation​.”

2021-01-13.title: Uganda Blocks Facebook Ahead of Contentious Election
2021-01-13.abstract: President Yoweri Museveni accused the company of “arrogance” after it removed fake accounts and pages linked to his re-election campaign.

2021-01-13.title: South Korean Church Leader Acquitted of Blocking Antivirus Efforts
2021-01-13.abstract: Lee Man-hee’s church, Shincheonji, was the epicenter of the first wave of coronavirus infections in South Korea last year.

2021-01-13.title: Aleksei Navalny Says He’ll Return to Russia on Sunday
2021-01-13.abstract: The opposition leader has been recovering in Germany from a poisoning attack widely attributed to  the Russian state.

2021-01-14.title: Two Members of W.H.O. Team on Trail of Virus Are Denied Entry to China
2021-01-14.abstract: Beijing has repeatedly impeded the global inquiry into the emergence of the coronavirus. Two scientists on the team remained in Singapore after testing positive for antibodies.

2021-01-14.abstract: Local authorities have refused to reopen an E.U.-funded housing facility for the migrants, bringing criticism that Bosnia has failed to provide basic humanitarian assistance required by international law.

2021-01-14.abstract: Bird song competitions — a pastime that is closer to meditation than to competitive sports — are big in Suriname. Success requires years of training and an appreciation for a slower pace of life.

2021-01-14.title: Infections remain comparatively rare among children but are rising, the C.D.C. says.
2021-01-14.abstract: After falling over the summer, coronavirus infections among children, teens and young adults rose steadily from September through mid-December, paralleling the virus’s trajectory among older adults in the U.S. population, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reported on Wednesday.

2021-01-14.abstract: Deaths from the coronavirus are skyrocketing in the United States, reaching levels never before seen, largely fueled by relentless surges in California and Arizona.

2021-01-15.title: Australia’s States Are Feuding Like Siblings. What Else to Do but Laugh?
2021-01-15.abstract: Australians are facing a messy patchwork of border restrictions that lock out both the coronavirus and fellow citizens. A new ad offers a biting, if humorous, commentary on the state of affairs.

2021-01-15.title: Mexico Exonerates Ex-Defense Chief Who Was Freed by the U.S.
2021-01-15.abstract: Gen. Salvador Cienfuegos Zepeda returned to Mexico last year after the Department of Justice abruptly dropped drug-trafficking and corruption charges.

2021-01-15.title: Joe the Pigeon Is Spared the Death Sentence in Australia
2021-01-15.abstract: A racing pigeon named after the president-elect appeared to have traveled from Oregon to Melbourne, breaching quarantine rules.

2021-01-15.abstract: The 6.2-magnitude earthquake, Indonesia’s third deadly disaster in a week, struck Sulawesi island, flattening a hospital, destroying homes and setting off landslides.

2021-01-15.title: Melvis Kwok, Tireless Elvis Impersonator in Hong Kong, Dies at 68
2021-01-15.abstract: Mr. Kwok, who busked in the Chinese territory for 28 years, was hardly the first Elvis Presley impersonator in Asia. But he may have been the most committed.

2021-01-16.title: India begins inoculating its 1.3 billion people, starting with health care workers.
2021-01-16.abstract: The vaccine campaign is unfolding in a country with more than 150,000 coronavirus deaths, the world’s third-highest tally.

2021-01-16.abstract: The five men fled by boat to Taiwan in July, soon after China imposed Hong Kong’s harsh national security law. This week, they landed in New York.

2021-01-16.title: In Hong Kong, a New Party Calls for Stability (and Raises Suspicions)
2021-01-16.abstract: Democracy advocates have called the Bauhinia Party a “Trojan horse” for the Chinese government. But Beijing’s local allies are wary of it, too.

2021-01-16.title: How British Scientists Found the More Infectious Coronavirus Variant
2021-01-16.abstract: Back in March, researchers decided to routinely record the genetic sequences of the virus they found, giving them a powerful tool for tracking mutations.

2021-01-16.abstract: Armin Laschet, a close ally of Germany’s chancellor, hopes to succeed her when she steps down after fall elections. But that is far from certain.

2021-01-17.title: 7 Dead in Military Helicopter Crash in Southern Philippines
2021-01-17.abstract: The helicopter had been on a supply run to troops in a remote, mountainous region where a hunt for communist rebels was taking place, the military said.

2021-01-17.title: Migrant Caravan, Now in Guatemala, Tests Regional Resolve to Control Migration
2021-01-17.abstract: As many as 7,000 migrants from Central America are hoping to reach the United States to escape poverty intensified by hurricanes and the pandemic. President-elect Joseph R.  Biden Jr. has pledged to ease asylum rules.

2021-01-17.abstract: A cave complex at a temple in Thailand has long drawn tourists, pilgrims and guano collectors. Now, scientists have arrived, looking for any potential links to the coronavirus.

2021-01-17.abstract: Aleksei Navalny, Russia’s most prominent opposition leader, landed in Russia Sunday night five months after he was poisoned with a nerve agent.

2021-01-17.abstract: The stockpile of doses was nearly empty.

2021-01-18.abstract: Mr. Navalny, the Russian opposition leader, was arrested at a Moscow airport after five months in Germany, recovering from a near-fatal poisoning.

2021-01-18.abstract: Lai Chi-wai didn’t reach his goal of ascending a skyscraper by rope. It hardly made his feat any less impressive.

2021-01-18.abstract: The European Union is eager for “a political climate change” and cooperation, but if the new president is consumed with domestic problems, it won’t put its own agenda on hold.

2021-01-19.abstract: After recovering from a near-fatal poisoning in Germany, the Russian opposition leader Aleksei A. Navalny returned to Moscow on Sunday and was detained at the airport.

2021-01-19.abstract: The sanctions are the first targeting the Nord Stream 2 undersea pipeline, which is to carry natural gas directly from Russia to Germany, whose government backs the project.

2021-01-19.title: Woman Is Sentenced to 43 Years for Criticizing Thai Monarchy
2021-01-19.abstract: Lawyers said it was the longest sentence yet for violating Thailand’s notoriously harsh lèse-majesté law.

2021-01-19.title: In Tunisia, Some Wonder if the Revolution Was Worth It
2021-01-19.abstract: Tunisians are putting their hard-won right to criticize the government to good use. They just wish there were less to protest.

2021-01-19.title: Pandemic teacher shortages imperil in-person schooling.
2021-01-19.abstract: Across the United States, state education and district officials say the pandemic has intensified a longstanding teacher shortage to crisis levels.

2021-01-20.title: Teenager’s Snow Cave Enters Canadian Survival Lore
2021-01-20.abstract: The tale of a British Columbia teenager’s canny survival instincts during a snowmobile outing has brought some pandemic cheer to Canada.

2021-01-20.title: China’s Oppression of Muslims in Xinjiang, Explained
2021-01-20.abstract: In one of its last acts, the Trump administration said China was committing genocide against Uighurs and other mostly Muslim groups. Here is what the move could mean.

2021-01-20.title: Here are steps you can take to protect yourself from the new coronavirus variant.
2021-01-20.abstract: A new variant of the coronavirus has caused concern in the United States because it’s so contagious and spreading fast. To avoid it, you’ll need to double down on the pandemic precautions that have kept you safe so far.

2021-01-20.title: Stinky strips of paper could, in theory, help drive down virus transmission.
2021-01-20.abstract: In a perfect world, the entrance to every office, restaurant and school would offer a coronavirus test — one with absolute accuracy that could instantly determine who was safe to admit and who should be turned away.

2021-01-20.title: Barbara Shelley, England’s leading lady of horror movies, dies at 88.
2021-01-20.abstract: Sometimes Barbara Shelley was the victim. By the end of the movie “Blood of the Vampire” (1958), the Victorian character she played — her brocade bodice properly ripped —was in chains in a mad scientist’s basement laboratory.

2021-01-21.title: Biden restores ties with the World Health Organization that were cut by Trump.
2021-01-21.abstract: Seeking to unify the global response to the coronavirus, President Biden on his first day in office retracted a decision by the Trump administration to withdraw from the World Health Organization.

2021-01-21.abstract: Asylum seekers detained in Australian hotels, deprived of sunlight and sometimes fresh air, had no idea how long their ordeal would last. For some, it’s now over.

2021-01-21.abstract: Such attacks, once common in the Iraqi capital, have become rarer in recent years as U.S.-backed Iraqi forces have largely defeated the Islamic State.

2021-01-21.title: During one pandemic, students are training for the next.

Their goal is to catalog the flu viruses that often lurk in urban fowl, some of which might have the potential to someday hop into humans.

2021-01-21.title: New coronavirus variants could challenge vaccine efforts.
2021-01-21.abstract: Confirmed coronavirus cases from new variants found first in Britain, then in South Africa, Brazil and the United States have people worried about whether vaccines can protect against altered versions of the virus. Experts said in interviews that so far vaccines are capable of providing that protection.

2021-01-22.title: Canada’s Governor General Resigns Amid Reports of a Toxic Workplace
2021-01-22.abstract: Julie Payette represented the queen as Canada’s official head of state, a high-profile but largely ceremonial role. She was accused of publicly humiliating employees.

2021-01-22.abstract: Donald J. Trump is not the only one departing.

2021-01-22.abstract: Margaret Court, an Australian record breaker known for her homophobic comments, is set to receive one of the nation’s highest public service awards.

2021-01-22.title: Dave Chappelle tests positive for the virus.
2021-01-22.abstract: The comedian canceled several upcoming shows in Austin, Texas, and is quarantining.

2021-01-23.title: Fresh Starts at the Top in Both Washington and Ottawa
2021-01-23.abstract: A day after Joseph R. Biden Jr. became president, resetting Canada’s relationship with its neighbor, Governor General Julie Payette quit as Queen Elizabeth’s representative as head of state.

2021-01-23.title: Long Lines as Beijing Starts Mass Coronavirus Testing
2021-01-23.abstract: The authorities in Beijing set up temporary testing sites after a spike in coronavirus infections. Long lines formed as the city tested nearly two million people on Friday.

2021-01-23.title: Beny Steinmetz, a Mining Magnate, Found Guilty in Swiss Corruption Trial
2021-01-23.abstract: Mr. Steinmetz, a French-Israeli businessman, was convicted on charges of corrupting foreign public officials in a bid to reap iron ore resources in Guinea. He was sentenced to five years in prison.

2021-01-23.title: Hong Kong and Madrid tighten lockdowns amid concerns over the new variant.
2021-01-23.abstract: New infections in recent months and a contagious new variant threatening to accelerate the pandemic have prompted some governments to revert to their harshest social-distancing measures.

2021-01-23.title: Philippine Drug Raid Leaves 13 Dead
2021-01-23.abstract: Officials said 12 suspects and a police officer were killed in the shootout, the bloodiest episode in years in President Rodrigo Duterte’s war on drugs.

2021-01-24.title: Chinese Miners Pulled to Surface 2 Weeks After Underground Explosion
2021-01-24.abstract: At least 11 miners who were trapped 2,000 feet below ground in the blast have been rescued. Others are still missing.

2021-01-24.title: Asia’s ‘El Chapo’ Is Arrested in Amsterdam
2021-01-24.abstract: Tse Chi Lop, said to be the leader of a multibillion-dollar drug syndicate, was arrested in Amsterdam and faces extradition to Australia.

2021-01-24.title: Pro-Navalny Protest Photos: Wave of Anger Rolls Across Russia
2021-01-24.abstract: Demonstrators in more than 100 cities rallied in support of the jailed opposition leader Aleksei A. Navalny in the biggest protests the nation has seen in years.

2021-01-24.title: Capitol Riot Puts Spotlight on ‘Apocalyptically Minded’ Global Far Right
2021-01-24.abstract: Leaderless but united by racist ideology that has been supercharged by social media, extremists have built a web of real and online connections that worries officials.

2021-01-24.title: For Nicaragua’s Lobstermen, Deadly Dives Are All Too Common
2021-01-24.abstract: Catching spiny lobsters is a stunningly dangerous pursuit for the mostly Indigenous fishermen along the country’s Caribbean coast, requiring deep plunges with subpar gear.

2021-01-25.title: Mexico’s president is the latest world leader to become infected with the coronavirus.
2021-01-25.abstract: Andrés Manuel López Obrador has scoffed at precautions to stop the spread of the coronavirus. Now he has it.

2021-01-25.title: Portugal’s President Wins Re-election, but Far Right Gains
2021-01-25.abstract: Portugal once stood out in Europe for having no real far-right presence in politics. Those days appear over.

2021-01-25.title: In Poor Madrid Neighborhood, Biggest Snowfall in Half Century Deepens Misery
2021-01-25.abstract: Life in Cañada Real, home to about 8,000 people, is “just as horrendous” as in Bosnian refugee camps, said one humanitarian aid worker who has visited both.

2021-01-25.title: China wanted to show off its vaccines. It’s backfiring.
2021-01-25.abstract: China’s coronavirus vaccines were supposed to deliver a geopolitical win that showcased the country’s scientific prowess and generosity. Instead, in some places, they have set off a backlash.

2021-01-25.title: Some homeless shelter operators in N.Y.C. bypass the city to speed up Wi-Fi access.
2021-01-25.abstract: Providing reliable internet access to the 111,000 children in homeless shelters and unstable housing in New York City has been one of the most stubborn obstacles to getting online schooling right, and for many students there’s no relief in sight. The city belatedly started putting Wi-Fi in 200 family shelters in November and says it won’t finish until the end of summer, after a second pandemic school year has come and gone.

2021-01-26.title: Mexicans Worry That Even Getting Covid Won’t Make Their Leader Take It Seriously
2021-01-26.abstract: President Andrés Manuel López Obrador has played down the pandemic, and many Mexicans wonder if his own illness will change his mind since he can count on the quality care that many struggle to find.

2021-01-26.title: See Which Country Is Leading the Global Race to Vaccinate
2021-01-26.abstract: Half of Israelis could receive at least one vaccine dose within a month, while the United States and Europe have been slower to reach their populations.

2021-01-26.title: As Angry Farmers Take to New Delhi’s Streets, Protests Turn Violent
2021-01-26.abstract: Protesters used their tractors to pull apart barricades, prompting the police to fire tear gas. At least one person died in the demonstration against new farming laws.

2021-01-26.title: Telegram, Pro-Democracy Tool, Struggles Over New Fans From Far Right
2021-01-26.abstract: The app has helped fuel democracy movements in Iran and Belarus but now faces scrutiny as extremists and conspiracy theorists flock to it amid crackdowns by Facebook and Twitter on disinformation.

2021-01-26.title: Mona Lisa Is Alone, but Still Smiling
2021-01-26.abstract: With the Louvre closed because of the pandemic, museum officials are pushing ahead on a grand restoration and cleanup.

2021-01-27.title: A Wisconsin pharmacist will plead guilty to attempting to spoil hundreds of vaccine doses.
2021-01-27.abstract: Authorities say Steven R. Brandenburg, a 46-year-old hospital pharmacist, attempted to spoil the doses of the Moderna vaccine by removing them from a hospital refrigeration unit.

2021-01-27.title: Vancouver Couple Fined After Flying to Remote Town for Vaccination
2021-01-27.abstract: The authorities charged an executive and an actress with violating coronavirus restrictions when they traveled to Beaver Creek in the Yukon Territory last week. Residents accused them of cutting the line.

2021-01-27.title: For sale in Oklahoma: a large stockpile of hydroxychloroquine.
2021-01-27.abstract: The Oklahoma Department of Health is still trying to unload a stockpile of hydroxychloroquine — an antimalarial drug touted by former President Donald J. Trump as a “miracle” cure for Covid-19 despite a lack of evidence that it effectively treats the disease — that it purchased for $2 million last spring.

2021-01-27.title: E.U. and U.K. Fighting Over Scarce Vaccines
2021-01-27.abstract: As vaccine production falls behind schedule, and the European Union lags in inoculating people, Brussels and London are lobbing threats and accusations at each other.

2021-01-27.title: Groups Put French State on Legal Notice Over Police Racism
2021-01-27.abstract: In a first, six nongovernmental organizations are taking legal steps to force an overhaul of the country’s policing.

2021-01-28.title: Thousands in Poland Protest Against Near-Total Abortion Ban
2021-01-28.abstract: Demonstrators flooded the streets after the sudden announcement that a ban on abortions for fetal abnormalities would take effect.

2021-01-28.title: Iran Angles for Advantage on Biden’s Priority List
2021-01-28.abstract: With a combination of threats, provocative acts and public appeals, Iran is jostling for attention from an administration preoccupied with spillover crises from the Trump years.

2021-01-28.title: E.U. Border Agency Pulls Out of Hungary Over Rights Abuses
2021-01-28.abstract: The decision by Frontex comes after Hungary disregarded a ruling by the E.U.’s top court that the way the country handles asylum seekers violated the bloc’s law.

2021-01-28.title: Thailand Legalizes Abortion in 1st Trimester but Keeps Other Restrictions
2021-01-28.abstract: Women who end a pregnancy after 12 weeks could still face prison or fines. Abortion rights advocates say more change is needed.

2021-01-28.title: To Avoid an Outbreak, China Cancels Lunar New Year for Millions of Migrants
2021-01-28.abstract: China has added restrictions, offered incentives and appealed to a sense of filial and national responsibility, in an effort to prevent about 300 million migrant workers from going home for the holiday.

2021-01-29.title: A Washington hospital system apologizes after offering vaccines to wealthy donors.
2021-01-29.abstract: A Washington hospital system apologized after The Seattle Times reported that it had offered vaccines to wealthy donors while others went without the coveted shots.

2021-01-29.title: Protests Continue in Poland After Near-Total Abortion Ban
2021-01-29.abstract: Demonstrators vowed to keep protesting Poland’s new abortion law, which went into effect Wednesday, tightening the country’s already restrictive laws.

2021-01-29.title: A ‘bunch of kids’ ran a major vaccination site for Philadelphia. Then the city cut its ties.
2021-01-29.abstract: With pressure mounting to get Covid-19 vaccine doses into arms as quickly as possible, many overburdened city health departments across the country have turned to partnerships with hospitals, nonprofit organizations and pharmacies. In Philadelphia this week, one such deal went awry after the city leaned on a start-up led by college students who were eager to get involved but had little experience.

2021-01-29.title: My Attempt to Prepare for a Future Without Google
2021-01-29.abstract: After the tech giant threatened to take its search engine offline in Australia, I’ve been looking into some alternatives.

2021-01-29.title: Tibetan Who Spoke Out for Language Rights Is Freed From Chinese Prison
2021-01-29.abstract: Tashi Wangchuk was sentenced to five years in prison after telling The New York Times that Tibetan language education was being repressed.

2021-01-30.title: Health Providers Worldwide Welcome Biden Reversal of Anti-Abortion Rule
2021-01-30.abstract: By restoring funding cut off by his predecessor, President Biden ended four years of what abortion rights advocates called a concerted assault on women’s reproductive health in the developing world.

2021-01-30.title: Coachella music festival is canceled again.
2021-01-30.abstract: The festivals were some of the first major events canceled back in April 2020 in the early days of the pandemic.

2021-01-30.title: After Newness Fades, Canada Settles Down to Legal Marijuana
2021-01-30.abstract: The national upheaval predicted when Canada became the first major industrialized nation to legalize recreational marijuana has not come to pass.

2021-01-30.title: The Australian Open will allow up to 30,000 spectators a day.
2021-01-30.abstract: Such numbers would make the tennis tournament a sports rarity during the pandemic, though attendance would still be down by about half from a normal year.

2021-01-30.title: As Protests Grip Russia, Putin Critics of Many Stripes Rally Around Navalny
2021-01-30.abstract: While many at the protests are critical of Aleksei A. Navalny, the Kremlin’s treatment of the opposition leader has united a disparate group of Putin’s opponents who say they can no longer tolerate official injustice.

2021-01-31.title: Protesters disrupt motorists from entering Dodger Stadium vaccination site.
2021-01-31.abstract: The closure lasted for about an hour before the entrance reopened, while vaccinations continued inside the stadium.

2021-01-31.title: Russia Protesters Defy Vast Police Operation as Signs of Kremlin Anxiety Mount
2021-01-31.abstract: Tens of thousands took to the streets across Russia to show support for the jailed opposition leader Aleksei A. Navalny. The police were out in force and reports of brutality flared.

2021-01-31.title: As Virus Variants Spread, ‘No One Is Safe Until Everyone Is Safe’
2021-01-31.abstract: Rich countries are buying up coronavirus vaccines, leaving poorer regions vulnerable — and as potential breeding grounds for variants, like one found in South Africa, that could make vaccines less effective.

2021-01-31.title: In Photos: Crowds of Police Couldn’t Quell Russia’s Pro-Navalny Protests
2021-01-31.abstract: More than 4,000 people were detained, but allies of the opposition leader Aleksei A. Navalny called Sunday's demonstrations a success.

2021-01-31.title: Russia’s Economic Slump Erodes Consensus That Shielded Putin
2021-01-31.abstract: The rally-around-the-flag effect of President Vladimir V. Putin’s assertive foreign policy known as the Crimea consensus is unraveling with the economy.
